articles.headline: Powerchip Reports Substantial Gross Margin Jump on Chip Price Hikes Amid Supply Shortage
articles.summary: Contract chipmaker Powerchip raised prices for display driver chips (30%), image sensors (20%), and 8-inch display driver chips (15%) since January, driving significant margin expansion. The company is expanding AI foundry services including advanced 3D packaging for HBM chips and IPDs, positioning itself to capture demand from the prolonged semiconductor supply crunch affecting high-performance computing and AI infrastructure.

articles.headline: Iran Energy Crisis Reshapes Global Clean Tech Competition, Beijing Strengthens Dominance
articles.summary: Geopolitical tensions and energy market disruptions are forcing countries to accelerate green transitions while managing concerns over China's clean-technology dominance. The supply chain volatility from sanctions and blockades is pushing businesses to prioritize renewable energy and diversified supply chains, reshaping long-term energy procurement strategies and infrastructure investment decisions.

articles.headline: Reserve Bank of Australia monitoring Anthropic's Mythos AI over cyberattack risks
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia is monitoring Anthropic's Mythos AI model, capable of enabling sophisticated cyberattacks, and engaging regulators and entities to assess impacts on financial system safety and resilience. This signals growing global regulatory focus on AI-driven cyber threats to critical infrastructure.
